verses.latin_vulgate: In die illa cantabitur canticum istud in terra Juda :  Urbs fortitudinis nostræ Sion ; salvator ponetur in ea  murus et antemurale.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֔וּא יוּשַׁ֥ר הַשִּׁיר־הַזֶּ֖ה בְּאֶ֣רֶץ יְהוּדָ֑ה עִ֣יר עׇז־לָ֔נוּ יְשׁוּעָ֥ה יָשִׁ֖ית חוֹמ֥וֹת וָחֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: In that day shall this song be sung in the land of Judah; We have a strong city; salvation will God appoint for walls and bulwarks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aperite portas, et ingrediatur gens justa,  custodiens veritatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פִּתְח֖וּ שְׁעָרִ֑ים וְיָבֹ֥א גוֹי־צַדִּ֖יק שֹׁמֵ֥ר אֱמֻנִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Open ye the gates, that the righteous nation which keepeth the truth may enter in.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vetus error abiit : servabis pacem ;  pacem, quia in te speravimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵ֣צֶר סָמ֔וּךְ תִּצֹּ֖ר שָׁל֣וֹם ׀ שָׁל֑וֹם כִּ֥י בְךָ֖ בָּטֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee: because he trusteth in thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sperastis in Domino in sæculis æternis ;  in Domino Deo forti in perpetuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּטְח֥וּ בַיהֹוָ֖ה עֲדֵי־עַ֑ד כִּ֚י בְּיָ֣הּ יְהֹוָ֔ה צ֖וּר עוֹלָמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Trust ye in the Lord for ever: for in the Lord Jehovah is everlasting strength:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia incurvabit habitantes in excelso ;  civitatem sublimem humiliabit :  humiliabit eam usque ad terram,  detrahet eam usque ad pulverem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י הֵשַׁח֙ יֹשְׁבֵ֣י מָר֔וֹם קִרְיָ֖ה נִשְׂגָּבָ֑ה יַשְׁפִּילֶ֤נָּה יַשְׁפִּילָהּ֙ עַד־אֶ֔רֶץ יַגִּיעֶ֖נָּה עַד־עָפָֽר׃
verses.king_james: For he bringeth down them that dwell on high; the lofty city, he layeth it low; he layeth it low, even to the ground; he bringeth it even to the dust.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conculcabit eam pes,  pedes pauperis, gressus egenorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּרְמְסֶ֖נָּה רָ֑גֶל רַגְלֵ֥י עָנִ֖י פַּעֲמֵ֥י דַלִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: The foot shall tread it down, even the feet of the poor, and the steps of the needy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Semita justi recta est,  rectus callis justi ad ambulandum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֹ֥רַח לַצַּדִּ֖יק מֵישָׁרִ֑ים יָשָׁ֕ר מַעְגַּ֥ל צַדִּ֖יק תְּפַלֵּֽס׃
verses.king_james: The way of the just is uprightness: thou, most upright, dost weigh the path of the just.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et in semita judiciorum tuorum, Domine, sustinuimus te :  nomen tuum et memoriale tuum in desiderio animæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֣ף אֹ֧רַח מִשְׁפָּטֶ֛יךָ יְהֹוָ֖ה קִוִּינ֑וּךָ לְשִׁמְךָ֥ וּלְזִכְרְךָ֖ תַּאֲוַת־נָֽפֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Yea, in the way of thy judgments, O Lord, have we waited for thee; the desire of our soul is to thy name, and to the remembrance of thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Anima mea desideravit te in nocte,  sed et spiritu meo in præcordiis meis de mane vigilabo ad te.  Cum feceris judicia tua in terra,  justitiam discent habitatores orbis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַפְשִׁ֤י אִוִּיתִ֙ךָ֙ בַּלַּ֔יְלָה אַף־רוּחִ֥י בְקִרְבִּ֖י אֲשַׁחֲרֶ֑ךָּ כִּ֞י כַּאֲשֶׁ֤ר מִשְׁפָּטֶ֙יךָ֙ לָאָ֔רֶץ צֶ֥דֶק לָמְד֖וּ יֹשְׁבֵ֥י תֵבֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: With my soul have I desired thee in the night; yea, with my spirit within me will I seek thee early: for when thy judgments are in the earth, the inhabitants of the world will learn righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misereamur impio, et non discet justitiam ;  in terra sanctorum iniqua gessit,  et non videbit gloriam Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֻחַ֤ן רָשָׁע֙ בַּל־לָמַ֣ד צֶ֔דֶק בְּאֶ֥רֶץ נְכֹח֖וֹת יְעַוֵּ֑ל וּבַל־יִרְאֶ֖ה גֵּא֥וּת יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Let favour be shewed to the wicked, yet will he not learn righteousness: in the land of uprightness will he deal unjustly, and will not behold the majesty of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, exaltetur manus tua, et non videant ;  videant, et confundantur zelantes populi ;  et ignis hostes tuos devoret.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָ֛ה רָ֥מָה יָדְךָ֖ בַּל־יֶחֱזָי֑וּן יֶחֱז֤וּ וְיֵבֹ֙שׁוּ֙ קִנְאַת־עָ֔ם אַף־אֵ֖שׁ צָרֶ֥יךָ תֹאכְלֵֽם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Lord, when thy hand is lifted up, they will not see: but they shall see, and be ashamed for their envy at the people; yea, the fire of thine enemies shall devour them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, dabis pacem nobis :  omnia enim opera nostra  operatus es nobis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָ֕ה תִּשְׁפֹּ֥ת שָׁל֖וֹם לָ֑נוּ כִּ֛י גַּ֥ם כׇּֽל־מַעֲשֵׂ֖ינוּ פָּעַ֥לְתָּ לָּֽנוּ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Lord, thou wilt ordain peace for us: for thou also hast wrought all our works in us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine Deus noster, possederunt nos  domini absque te ;  tantum in te recordemur nominis tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֵ֔ינוּ בְּעָל֥וּנוּ אֲדֹנִ֖ים זוּלָתֶ֑ךָ לְבַד־בְּךָ֖ נַזְכִּ֥יר שְׁמֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: O Lord our God, other lords beside thee have had dominion over us: but by thee only will we make mention of thy name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Morientes non vivant,  gigantes non resurgant :  propterea visitasti et contrivisti eos,  et perdidisti omnem memoriam eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵתִים֙ בַּל־יִ֣חְי֔וּ רְפָאִ֖ים בַּל־יָקֻ֑מוּ לָכֵ֤ן פָּקַ֙דְתָּ֙ וַתַּשְׁמִידֵ֔ם וַתְּאַבֵּ֥ד כׇּל־זֵ֖כֶר לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.king_james: They are dead, they shall not live; they are deceased, they shall not rise: therefore hast thou visited and destroyed them, and made all their memory to perish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Indulsisti genti, Domine,  indulsisti genti, numquid glorificatus es ?  elongasti omnes terminos terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָסַ֤פְתָּ לַגּוֹי֙ יְהֹוָ֔ה יָסַ֥פְתָּ לַגּ֖וֹי נִכְבָּ֑דְתָּ רִחַ֖קְתָּ כׇּל־קַצְוֵי־אָֽרֶץ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Thou hast increased the nation, O Lord, thou hast increased the nation: thou art glorified: thou hadst removed it far unto all the ends of the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, in angustia requisierunt te,  in tribulatione murmuris doctrina tua eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָ֖ה בַּצַּ֣ר פְּקָד֑וּךָ צָק֣וּן לַ֔חַשׁ מוּסָרְךָ֖ לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Lord, in trouble have they visited thee, they poured out a prayer when thy chastening was upon them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut quæ concipit, cum appropinquaverit ad partum,  dolens clamat in doloribus suis,  sic facti sumus a facie tua, Domine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּמ֤וֹ הָרָה֙ תַּקְרִ֣יב לָלֶ֔דֶת תָּחִ֥יל תִּזְעַ֖ק בַּחֲבָלֶ֑יהָ כֵּ֛ן הָיִ֥ינוּ מִפָּנֶ֖יךָ יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Like as a woman with child, that draweth near the time of her delivery, is in pain, and crieth out in her pangs; so have we been in thy sight, O Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Concepimus, et quasi parturivimus,  et peperimus spiritum.  Salutes non fecimus in terra ;  ideo non ceciderunt habitatores terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָרִ֣ינוּ חַ֔לְנוּ כְּמ֖וֹ יָלַ֣דְנוּ ר֑וּחַ יְשׁוּעֹת֙ בַּל־נַ֣עֲשֶׂה אֶ֔רֶץ וּבַֽל־יִפְּל֖וּ יֹשְׁבֵ֥י תֵבֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: We have been with child, we have been in pain, we have as it were brought forth wind; we have not wrought any deliverance in the earth; neither have the inhabitants of the world fallen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vivent mortui tui,  interfecti mei resurgent.  Expergiscimini, et laudate,  qui habitatis in pulvere,  quia ros lucis ros tuus,  et terram gigantum detrahes in ruinam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִֽחְי֣וּ מֵתֶ֔יךָ נְבֵלָתִ֖י יְקוּמ֑וּן הָקִ֨יצוּ וְרַנְּנ֜וּ שֹׁכְנֵ֣י עָפָ֗ר כִּ֣י טַ֤ל אוֹרֹת֙ טַלֶּ֔ךָ וָאָ֖רֶץ רְפָאִ֥ים תַּפִּֽיל׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Thy dead men shall live, together with my dead body shall they arise. Awake and sing, ye that dwell in dust: for thy dew is as the dew of herbs, and the earth shall cast out the dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vade, populus meus, intra in cubicula tua ;  claude ostia tua super te,  abscondere modicum ad momentum,  donec pertranseat indignatio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֤ךְ עַמִּי֙ בֹּ֣א בַחֲדָרֶ֔יךָ וּֽסְגֹ֥ר (דלתיך) [דְּלָֽתְךָ֖] בַּעֲדֶ֑ךָ חֲבִ֥י כִמְעַט־רֶ֖גַע עַד־(יעבור)־[יַעֲבׇר־]זָֽעַם׃
verses.king_james: Come, my people, enter thou into thy chambers, and shut thy doors about thee: hide thyself as it were for a little moment, until the indignation be overpast.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce enim Dominus egredietur de loco suo,  ut visitet iniquitatem habitatoris terræ contra eum ;  et revelabit terra sanguinem suum, et non operiet ultra interfectos suos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־הִנֵּ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ יֹצֵ֣א מִמְּקוֹמ֔וֹ לִפְקֹ֛ד עֲוֺ֥ן יֹֽשֵׁב־הָאָ֖רֶץ עָלָ֑יו וְגִלְּתָ֤ה הָאָ֙רֶץ֙ אֶת־דָּמֶ֔יהָ וְלֹא־תְכַסֶּ֥ה ע֖וֹד עַל־הֲרוּגֶֽיהָ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: For, behold, the Lord cometh out of his place to punish the inhabitants of the earth for their iniquity: the earth also shall disclose her blood, and shall no more cover her slain.
